addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (Prefer the skills shipped with the Figma plugin on the agent being used. If no Figma plugin is installed, use the skills served by the Figma MCP resource):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-use/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-design/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-library/SKILL.md
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components; fallback: skill://figma/figma-code-connect/SKILL.md
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_f… [truncated]

content.content:
===== FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md (tail 40) =====
- 2026-07-12 | Rank4a AUDIT INSTRUMENT | Built `scripts/flywheel_topic_judge_audit.py` — re-runs the SAME judge (same model/rq/batch) read-only, deletes nothing, and dumps EVERY row's verdict + tier + title + the prior retrieval-time demote label, so the judge itself can be graded and cross-checked against the independent earlier judge. This is the material the Fable gate needs; grading a deletion policy on aggregate percentages alone is how a false hard-drop gets waved through.

- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE (Rank4) | Verdict **PASS-WITH-FIX** — and it **REFUTED TWO OF MY OWN CLAIMS**, both of which I had already put in writing. (i) My headline "**100%** of the fresh OFF_SUBJECT set was corroborated by the independent earlier judge" was **a bug in my own audit instrument**: `classify_topic_relevance` rewrites `topic_offtopic_demoted` **on the row objects IN PLACE** (topic_relevance_gate.py:679,685, plus the default-ON rescue-on-stamp leg), so my script — which read that field back *after* judging — was comparing the judge **against itself**. Circular. Verified the mutation myself, recomputed against the pristine on-disk corpus: **the honest number is 99.2% (529/533)**, with exactly 4 uncorroborated rows (ev_1032, ev_420, ev_588, ev_621). (ii) "Two independent **questions**" overstated the evidence — the corpus RQ and task-72 are near-identical in subject+aspect, so it is two independent **invocations** (rules out a one-off parse/model fault; does NOT prove question-independence). Fable also *raised* my confidence where it counted: it graded a 64-row stratified sample and found **~83% manifestly alien, ~1.6% clear false positives**, and showed the fresh judge **CORRECTS the prior judge's false positives** (it verdicts ON_TOPIC the seminal rows the retrieval-time pass had wrongly demoted: "GPTs are GPTs", "Generative AI at Work", "Labor Demand in the Age of Generative AI").
- 2026-07-12 | Rank4 LANDED (d3bd320) | Committed the arming + 3 fixes. **The important one: the containment rail was SELF-CONCEALING** — on a ceiling trip the disclosure wrote `off_subject_ev_ids: []`, i.e. the rail **discarded the 529 ids that were the only evidence capable of distinguishing a broken judge from an alien corpus**. A broken judge and a genuinely alien corpus left byte-identical artifacts. Now persisted as `refused_off_subject_ev_ids` (REFUSED-and-KEPT, never conflated with deleted). Also: rescued/refused rows kept a live `topic_off_subject=True` (the re-judge ran on `dict(row)`, a COPY, so the gate's stale-stamp pop never reached the original — inert today, a landmine for any future snapshot write); and the ceiling denominator counted exempt+chrome rows the judge never verdicts, so the rail tripped LATER than its setting reads. **Deliberately NOT done: the ceiling is NOT raised.** With a ~50%-alien corpus, *any* fraction ceiling below ~55% refuses forever — the instrument structurally cannot answer the question it is being asked. Fable's discriminator is the right one and is the next step: **a broken judge does not reproduce row-level verdicts across independently shuffled passes; an alien corpus does.**
- 2026-07-12 | Rank1/2/2b VERIFIED END-TO-END | The control run (junk gate live, judge OFF) dumped its outline: log says `JUNK-GATE (§-1.3.1a): deleted 53 chrome non-source(s)`, and the **"Just a moment..." Cloudflare rows cited as evidence fell 56 -> 3**.        ~51%-off-topic corpus produces kept-but-IRRELEVANT prose, never dropped prose,
       and cannot be what kept_fraction=0.35 measures. My thesis was wrong.
    
    2. THERE IS NO LENGTH GOVERNOR ON THIS PATH. PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS=8000, the
       expansion passes, PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION and the token-budget tiering are
       consumed only by agents/synthesizer.py + synthesis/section_writer.py, which
       multi_section_generator.py does not import (grep: 0 hits); compose calls

The property that DOES separate them: **a broken judge does not reproduce ROW-LEVEL verdicts across independently shuffled passes; an alien corpus does** (an alien row is alien regardless of which rows it is batched with). MEASURED: re-judging the 533 candidates in **shuffled batch company reproduced 518 — 97.2% row-level agreement.** The verdicts are a property of the ROW, not the batch. **The corpus is alien; the judge is not broken.** And the 15 that FLAPPED were **exactly the boundary rows Fable had predicted as false positives, in an experiment it never saw**: ev_1247 (over-reliance on AI dialogue systems — AI×education), ev_1032 (AI in Science, OECD), ev_1047 + ev_636 (BLS employment projections / Employment Situation Summary — real labour statistics), ev_776 (T1, open innovation × Industry 4.0). A mechanism confirming a prediction it was not fitted to is the strongest evidence available here.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 LANDED (b1f3f68) | Deletion now requires affirmative OFF_SUBJECT in **BOTH independent shuffled passes, at EVERY tier** (the old rail escalated only T1/T2, leaving the same boundary noise unchecked everywhere else); any disagreement KEEPS. T1/T2 escalation retained **on top** as a third look (can only rescue). **SAME-WORK GUARD** added: never delete a row whose same-work sibling survived ON_TOPIC — and note the bug I caught by checking the real data shape: `same_work_groups` arrive as **dicts** with `member_evidence_ids`, not bare id lists, so my first cut would have **silently matched nothing**. A safety rail that quietly no-ops is worse than no rail. The fraction ceiling is **reclassified from policy knob to OUTAGE TRIPWIRE (0.75)** — it now catches only the failure a per-row rule cannot (a systemic fault where BOTH passes are garbage and agree), and can still only ever REFUSE, never widen a delete set. **NET (offline dry-run vs recorded verdicts): 518 both-pass − 5 same-work = 513 deletable, 484 kept, ZERO seminal papers deleted** (Acemoglu/Autor/Brynjolfsson, "GPTs are GPTs", "Generative AI at Work" all survive).
- 2026-07-12 | DEPTH RISK, MEASURED HONESTLY (free, no LLM) | Deletion is **NOT depth-free**: the residual catch-all loses 64% of its rows, but **thematic sections lose 35%**, and two lose most of their evidence (Policy Implications −77%, Skill Transformation −62%). I looked at what is actually being cut there rather than trusting the percentage: **job postings** ("Front End Web Developer, Growth Lab"; "DATA ENTRY CLERK, Omni Interactions, Denver, CO"), "15 Part-Time Jobs for Retirees (No Degree Required!)", "Recommended Reads for Paralegals", "Decomposing Climate Risks in Stock Markets", "The Power of Artists' Books". Those sections were **padded** by the Rank3 lexical router smearing alien rows in on one-word matches ("work"/"jobs"). So the −77% is a loss of PADDING, not depth. **492 genuine thematic rows survive = the real depth budget.** Honest consequence: the report gets **shorter but truer**, and the 4k-vs-10-22k word gap must now be closed by GENERATION DEPTH on real evidence — not by padding with job listings. That reframes the mission's biggest open gap.

- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 ARMED RUN — **THE JUDGE DELETED FOR THE FIRST TIME** | Full gate fired end-to-end on the real corpus and **reproduced the offline experiment exactly**: pass A = 533 candidates (a THIRD independent invocation landing on 533 — 529/533/533), **CROSS-PASS 518/533 held (97.2%)** — identical to the standalone, because the fixed seed makes pass B deterministic — same-work guard −4, T1/T2 escalation rescued 3 more. **VERDICT: 511/944 rows (54.1%) DELETABLE**, under the new 75% outage tripwire. Note the denominator is now **944, not 997** — the judged-rows fix is live. And `ev_1247` (Fable's #1 named false positive) was rescued **again**, this time by the T1/T2 escalation: a **third independent mechanism** catching the same row.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 outline CRASHED — **not my code** | After the judge succeeded, the run died in the OUTLINE agent: `ReasoningFirstTruncationError` — the reasoning-first outline model (deepseek-v4-pro) overran its budget and the LLM client **refused to promote truncated reasoning as content** ("Promoting truncated reasoning-derived text as verified prose would ship the model's scratchpad"). That is a pre-existing **fail-loud safety guard doing its job**, not a regression: the control run never tripped it, the judge verdict was fully captured, and nothing was corrupted. Retrying with `PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=49152` / `PG_OUTLINE_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=24576`. **Deliberately did NOT add a judge-verdict cache** to avoid re-paying the ~13min judge on retry — a stale cached verdict is exactly the failure class §-1.3.1 forbids, and saving 13 minutes is not worth reintroducing it.

- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 END-TO-END — **THE JUDGE DELETED, AND THE DELETION IS CLEAN** | Retry (bigger outline budget) ran the whole gate: pass A 530 candidates -> **CROSS-PASS 514/530 = 97.0% held** (vs 97.2% the run before — the discriminator is *stable across independent runs*) -> same-work guard −5 -> T1/T2 escalation rescued 2 more -> **VERDICT 507/944 (53.7%) DELETED**. Pool seam applied it: `JUNK-GATE: deleted 556 rows` (507 off-topic + 49 chrome). Outline built on the clean pool: **11 sections, 441 rows**. And `ev_1132` ("Hybrid working from home improves retention" — the row **I** had flagged as a likely false positive, labour-but-not-AI) was **rescued by the escalation**: my own doubt caught by the machinery rather than by hand. Likewise `ev_1270` (Fable's other named FP) flapped and was kept.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 VERIFICATION — **0 judge-kept rows lost** | Guarded against the failure that would matter most: **of the 490 corpus rows the judge KEPT, ZERO are missing from the armed outline.** The deletion did exactly what it claimed and nothing more. Residual catch-all share fell **36% -> 26%**, and the outliner produced *more specific* sections on the clean pool ("Measuring Occupational Exposure to Generative AI", "Aggregate Employment and Displacement Effects", "Global Divides and Sectoral Heterogeneity") instead of the generic ones. **CAUTION — I nearly misread my own comparator:** it reported "723 deleted, 112 high-tier" — but the CONTROL outline has **1164 rows against a 997-row corpus**, because the outliner does **live gap-fill retrieval** (220 rows never in the corpus). Diffing two outlines built from different retrievals is not a deletion count. The real number is 507, and the real safety check is the zero-kept-rows-lost one above.
- 2026-07-12 | OPEN CONFOUND (next lever) | The control gap-filled **220 live-retrieved rows**; the armed run gap-filled **0**. So the two outlines are not strictly size-comparable, and more importantly: **a cleaner, smaller pool should make gap-fill retrieval MORE valuable, not less.** If the outliner is skipping retrieval when the pool shrinks, that is a bug and it is also exactly the lever for the known depth gap (~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k) — the honest way to regain length is *fresh on-topic evidence*, not the job listings and paralegal reading lists we just removed. **This is the next thing to chase.**

## Net so far
**52 failed fetches (Cloudflare/ResearchGate challenge cards) were grounding report prose as citable T7 "sources"; they are now deleted and disclosed.** The faithfulness engine could never have caught this — strict_verify checks sentence↔span fidelity, and a sentence *can* be entailed by the text of a challenge card, so chrome passed the only hard gate and still grounded prose on nothing.

Regression: 75 failed / 2269 passed vs **77 / 2266 at pre-flywheel baseline (0e0eca9)** — zero new failures; the 75 are pre-existing and unrelated (audit_ir registry + import-path collection errors).

## Next
- Re-run outline-only (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1) to confirm the 52 rows are gone end-to-end, then Fable-gate the new outline for richness before any compose.
- Open quality gap (still unaddressed, from the laptop handoff): verified MATH/STAT synthesis (the outliner collects numbers but does not compute them), and report length ~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k.
- The 4 sciencedirect rows share the stale "Just a moment..." TITLE but have genuinely recovered bodies — 3 are correctly KEPT, but their **title is still chrome** and will render into the bibliography as "Just a moment...". Title repair (NOT deletion) is a separate small step.

- 2026-07-12 | Rank6 DIAGNOSIS — **REFUTED BY FABLE, AND MY EVIDENCE WAS A MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT** | I claimed the outline agent's gap-fill depth arm was dead: the checklist's three arms (coverage|density|numeric_rows) all pass through `_quote_is_grounded` (outline_agent.py:1454) BEFORE `kind` is parsed (:1458), so a DENSITY gap ("this section has too few sources") must quote the QUESTION verbatim — which a section's thinness never names. Cited "28 coverage / 1 numeric_rows / **0 density** across every log ever". **That count was garbage:** the accepted-gap disclosure (:1506-1509) logs only `section::aspect` and **never logs `kind`** — so the only kind-tagged lines in any log are the ones echoed inside `dropped N ungrounded line(s)`. I counted the REJECTS and reported them as the population. Fable caught it, and found the stronger fact underneath: **zero density lines appear even among the DROPPED lines — the model never emits them at all.** So the suppressor is the PROMPT (density is defined as "backed by only 1-2 sources" (:1322) while every worked example is `coverage`), not the code order — and my headline fix (reorder the parse) would have been a **no-op**.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank6 KILLED — **THE DEPTH CEILING IS COMPOSE, NOT RETRIEVAL** | Fable's decisive finding, verified by me against the source: word count is architecturally fixed and **evidence-independent**. `multi_section_generator.py:4` states the contract outright — *"produces 1500-3000-word reports"* — and the section writer's rule 8 (:3564, :3713) sets a **fixed** *"Target 10-18 sentences"* per section regardless of payload. 11 sections x 10-18 sentences x ~22 words = **2.4-4.4k words = exactly our observed ~4k.** Pushing MORE rows into sections (what Rank6 would have done) is the direction this project **already measured as WORSE**: WHEEL_PROGRESS.md:259-261 — *"THIN body (2141 words vs step3 3230) ... kept_fraction=0.35 -> 102 dropped sentences (step3=64), 77 verified (step3=91)"*. More rows drowned strict_verify and made the report SHORTER. **Rank6 was aimed at the wrong stage.**
- 2026-07-12 | Rank6a LATENT HOLE (found by Fable, verified by me) | My safety argument — "gap-fill rows are screened by the same armed topic judge at fold-in" — is **half-false in the half that matters**. `_fold_in.py:120` reads `topic_result.dropped_rows`, but `classify_topic_relevance` hard-codes `dropped_rows, dropped_titles = [], []` (topic_relevance_gate.py:709) unless `PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP=1`, **which defaults to "0"** (:109). The chrome screen is real; **the off-topic screen at fold-in cannot delete anything.** Rank5's deletion is a *different* path that runs on the pre-built corpus BEFORE the outline loop, so live-retrieved rows never face it. Any future increase in retrieval MUST arm this first or it re-imports the exact alien rows Rank5 removed. Logged, not yet fixed — nothing currently depends on it (gap-fill fetched 0 rows this run).
- 2026-07-12 | THE MISSING MEASUREMENT (what Fable's ceiling argument does NOT settle) | Every compose measurement this project has ever taken — including the `kept_fraction=0.35` collapse that proves "more rows => thinner prose" — was taken on the **DIRTY corpus, the one that was ~50% alien**. The writer's menu held job listings, paralegal reading lists and Cloudflare captchas; it wrote sentences from them; strict_verify correctly killed them. **That is what kept_fraction=0.35 IS.** Rank5 removed precisely those rows — and **we have never once composed a report from the clean pool** (Rank5 stopped at outline-only). So the ceiling's own load-bearing number is confounded: on clean evidence the same fixed 10-18-sentence target should SURVIVE verification at a far higher rate, i.e. more verified words from the same prompt. Composing the clean pool is the cheapest experiment that can distinguish "compose is a hard architectural ceiling" from "compose was being fed garbage", and it must be run BEFORE any compose-stage change is designed. Launching it now.

- 2026-07-12 21:30 | RESUMED after reboot; GitHub push BLOCKED (no PAT/SSH/gh on box) -> fell back to bundle backup /home/polaris/backups/flywheel-v1-b1f3f68.bundle (183MB, HEAD b1f3f68). **Operator: paste a PAT and I will push.** The two clean-pool composes launched pre-reboot SURVIVED (orphaned to PID 1) and are the right experiment: armed(54370, PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE=1) vs ctrl(54371, judge off), same corpus cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json, task 72, separate worktrees.
- 2026-07-12 21:30 | NEAR-MISS, SELF-CAUGHT (the Rank6 error pattern, avoided) | I read `topic_gate DEMOTED(kept, disclosed) 610 off-topic source(s)` + found 42 alien titles still physically present in the corpus JSON, and concluded the A/B was NULL (both arms dirty) and was about to KILL both runs. **That conclusion was false.** The DEMOTE line is the *scope gate* (topic_relevance_gate.py:709 hard-codes dropped_rows=[] unless PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP=1) and it runs in BOTH arms — it is not the arm under test. The armed arm's actual mechanism is a DIFFERENT path: `_arm_topic_judge` (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:201), the Rank4/Rank5 two-pass semantic judge, which logs `ARMED: judging 997 rows ... only an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT deletes` -> `PASS B: re-judging the 526 candidates SHUFFLED (seed=1729), deletable = OFF_SUBJECT in BOTH passes`. Ctrl logs ZERO judge lines. The judge deletes **in-memory at load**, which is why the alien rows remain in the corpus FILE — the file is not the pool. **Lesson (same as Rank6): a grep of a log is not a measurement of a mechanism. Two mechanisms shared one word ("topic") and I almost destroyed the one experiment that matters.** Runs left untouched.
- 2026-07-12 21:40 | GROUNDING READ of baseline_routed_outline.json (8 sections, 1032 routed rows) — **THE CONFOUND IS NOW MECHANICAL, NOT SPECULATIVE.** Cross-referencing the prior armed judge's verdicts (outputs/agentic_report_20260712_203630/topic_judge_dispositions.json: n_in=997, n_off_subject_deletable=**507 = 51%**) against the routed outline shows the off-subject rows are **NOT quarantined in the catch-all — they are spread through EVERY real section**: sec1 Introduction 50.0% off-subject, sec2 Historical Patterns 57.3%, sec5 Polarization/Wages 50.0%, sec7 Conclusions 55.2%, sec8 "Additional Corroborated Findings" 57.2%; only sec3 (Occupational Exposure, 13.4%) is genuinely clean. **So the section writer composes its fixed 10-18 sentences from a menu that is ~half alien, strict_verify correctly kills the alien-derived sentences, and THAT is what kept_fraction=0.35 is.** This is exactly the confound the ceiling argument rests on, now with a per-section number. (I first hypothesised the router *quarantines* junk into the sec8 dump — the data REFUTED my own hypothesis; junk is everywhere.)
- 2026-07-12 21:40 | SECOND FINDING (independent of the A/B): **TIER IS NOT RELEVANCE — a tier-based "quality firewall" would be actively harmful.** 56 of the dump bucket's rows are T1/T2 (highest credibility) and flagly off-topic: a gradeless-learning RCT (T1), a mass-deworming study (T1), Wiley's *Regulation & Governance* (T2), a Chicago Quantitative Alliance reading list (T2). The codebase already knows this (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:155-160: *"tier is NOT a keep-veto ... a blanket high-tier exemption would shield precisely the credible-tier junk this audit found"*). **This retires the operator's proposed experiment #1 (SOURCE-ELIGIBILITY FIREWALL on tier/peer-reviewed/English) as originally specified: gating on tier-a prior would have KEPT all 56 of those rows and deleted nothing that matters.** Relevance, not credibility, is the axis that is broken — and the two-pass semantic judge already on the bench is the instrument for it. Structural depth gap also logged: the synthesis section ("Cross-Study Synthesis: Convergences and Contradictions"), the single most RACE-depth-bearing section, is STARVED at 14 rows while the catch-all holds 545.
- 2026-07-12 21:50 | **FABLE GATE: LOCK A *REFUTED* — MY CONFOUND THESIS WAS WRONG, AND THE REAL CEILING IS NOW NAMED.** I argued the ~51%-alien menu was what strict_verify was killing (=> kept_fraction 0.35, => clean pool would grow the report for free). **strict_verify is TOPICALITY-BLIND.** `strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool)` (provenance_generator.py:3598) never takes the research question, and its NLI judge is `judge(self, sentence, span)` (entailment_judge.py:740) prompted *"Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE"* (:584) — no question, no topic, no section title. **A sentence faithfully written from a real-but-ALIEN source is entailed by its span and is KEPT.** Off-topic evidence therefore produces kept-but-IRRELEVANT sentences, never dropped ones — so it cannot be what kept_fraction measures. (Verified all 3 claims myself against source.) The clean-pool A/B still running is now a FALSIFICATION TEST of Fable's prediction: armed kept_fraction should move <=±0.05. Its real payoff was never length — it is the RELEVANCE of shipped prose, i.e. a RACE-score win.
- 2026-07-12 21:50 | **THE ACTUAL CEILING (Fable, verified): THERE IS NO LENGTH GOVERNOR ON THIS PATH AT ALL.** `PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS=8000`, `PG_SYNTHESIS_MAX_EXPANSION_PASSES`, `PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION`, `PG_SECTION_TOKEN_BUDGET`, `PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS` are consumed ONLY by `agents/synthesizer.py` + `synthesis/section_writer.py`, reached via graph.py:1995 -> synthesize_report. **`multi_section_generator.py` imports NEITHER (grep: 0 hits)** and compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:594 calls `generate_multi_section_report` directly. Every word-budget knob in .env is DEAD CODE on the flywheel path; :11646 only *sums* total_words as telemetry. **Report length is purely emergent: n_sections x rule-#8 sentence target x ~30 w/sentence, minus B11 dedup.** Measured on control_report.md (3875w): evidence-rich sections already render 16-18 sentences = AT the top of rule #8's band, i.e. **the target is binding exactly where evidence is rich.** Corrected ceiling ~9 x 18 x 30 = 4.9k. Frontier = 10-22k. Section count is NOT the bound (facet outline allows 40; we use 9).
- 2026-07-12 21:50 | ALSO: kept_fraction=0.35 was a **MENU-SIZE** artifact, not a topicality one — multi_section_generator.py:513-527 records it was measured with PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD ON, which ballooned section menus to **52-103 rows** (healthy step3 = ~15); the writer was crammed and 65% of what it wrote failed span-binding. **The purpose-built fix, `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (:541), caps the writer's prompt menu and has NEVER been switched on — it appears only in tests.** Our compose script sets both of the flags that caused the collapse (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:530-531) and does not set the cap.
- 2026-07-12 22:00 | **Rank7 COMMITTED (33108d8): the depth ceiling is ONE PROMPT LINE, and it is now a lever.** Added `PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET` + `PG_SECTION_DISTINCT_SOURCES`, applied at the single `_select_section_system_prompt` seam (multi_section_generator.py:3903) so it CANNOT half-fire across the clinical (:3564) + field-agnostic (:3713) templates — editing one alone is a silent 50% no-op. Unset => the original template OBJECT is returned (byte-identical; `assert t is base`). Drifted anchor RAISES instead of silently no-opping. **My own test caught a real defect mid-build:** rewriting rule #8 alone left the Mechanism rule's back-reference ("rule #8 target of 10-18 sentences applies as usual", :3611) pointing at the OLD band — the model would have been handed "28-36" and "10-18" in the same prompt. Now rewritten too, with a hard assert that no stale "10-18" survives. Rule #9 (distinct-source floor) moves WITH rule #8 by design: **B11 same-span dedup (verified_compose.dedup_same_span_sentences, default ON) keeps only ONE sentence per distinct (ev_id,start,end) footprint**, so extra sentences that re-word an already-cited span are DELETED — the lever is "cite more DISTINCT sources", not "write more sentences". 9 new tests pass; 36 pass across the neighbouring prompt-selector suites; the 40 collection errors are the pre-existing audit_ir import breakage. NOT yet run end-to-end.

Now I have true state. Two important things — one of which changes your instructions:

**Your proposed experiment #1 (source-eligibility firewall on tier/peer-review) was already retired by data at 21:40**, and I shouldn't re-run it. The reason: tier is not relevance. 56 rows in the junk bucket are T1/T2 — a mass-deworming RCT (T1), a gradeless-learning RCT (T1), a Chicago Quantitative Alliance reading list (T2). The codebase already knows this (`compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:155-160` says outright "tier is NOT a keep-veto"). Relevance, not credibility, is the broken axis — and the two-pass semantic judge already on the bench is the right instrument for it.

The live frontier is **Rank7**, committed but never run end-to-end. Let me check the two clean-pool composes that were orphaned by the reboot.

Both runs are healthy and in different stages: **ctrl** is already composing sections; **armed** re-ran the judge and reproduced **507 deletable — exactly the same number as the previous independent run** (that's a fourth independent invocation landing on the same verdict), and is now in outline gap-fill.

One live risk I can see in the armed log right now: it *is* gap-filling this time (`routed 1/2/4 new ev_id(s)`, search fold-in), which means the **Rank6a latent hole is now load-bearing** — `_fold_in.py:120` reads `dropped_rows`, which `topic_relevance_gate.py:709` hard-codes to `[]` unless `PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP=1`. So the freshly retrieved rows face the chrome screen but **not** the off-topic judge. The armed arm may be re-importing alien rows through the back door. I'll measure that rather than assume it.

Let me log the state and check the code path while the runs finish.

content.content:
# ``pop(..., None)`` REMOVES a stale True but is a no-op on a clean row (key never
        # present) — so a fresh non-OFF_SUBJECT row stays sidecar-ABSENT (the contract downstream
        # and the tests both assert absence, not False). Guarded by ``split`` so
        # PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT=0 is byte-identical (legacy path never writes it).
        if split:
            for row in offaspect_rows:
                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
            for row in ontopic_rows:
                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
        dropped_rows, dropped_titles = [], []
        demoted_rows, demoted_titles = offtopic_rows, offtopic_titles

    verb = "dropped_offtopic" if hard_drop else "demoted_offtopic"
    notes = [
        f"topic_gate: in={n_in} kept={len(kept_rows)} "
        f"{verb}={len(offtopic_rows)} exempt={len(exempt_rows)} "
=== fold_in 112-126 ===
        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
        )
        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
        )
        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
        deleted_offtopic = []
        for row in dropped:
            copied = dict(row)
            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic

content.text:
Careful here — the script printed "PREDICTION BROKEN" mechanically, but **the statistics don't support that claim**, and this is exactly the Rank6 error pattern I need to avoid. kept_fraction is a proportion over ~160 sentence trials; binomial standard error on the *difference* is ≈0.055, so a +0.085 delta is only ~1.5σ. That is **not** a refutation — it's consistent with noise from a single run per arm.

What is *not* noise is the headline: **the clean pool produced a 32% longer report (3,408 → 4,489 words) with 104 verified sentences vs 81**, and 72 bibliography entries vs 63.

The two things that actually matter now are free to measure: the Rank6a leak, and the *relevance* of shipped prose (the real payoff Fable named).

content.text:
That's a genuinely surprising result, and it **refutes an assumption this project has been carrying for hours** — including my own framing an hour ago.

The dirty pool was **~51% alien, yet its shipped bibliography is only 1.6% alien** (1 of 63 rows). The alien rows almost never reach the report's citations. So the "relevance of shipped prose" payoff that Fable named as the real prize is worth **about one citation** — it is essentially zero.

The gain is real, but it's in a different place than anyone predicted: **depth**. Armed produced 104 verified sentences vs 81. Let me find the actual mechanism rather than assume one.

content.text:
Found the mechanism. There's a per-section `kept_fraction` floor with retry (`post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.31 below min 0.40 — retrying`), and the clean pool raised the writer's **repair success from 0.71 → 0.87**. So cleanliness helps the *writer bind sentences to spans*, not the citation relevance — the writer was already avoiding alien rows when choosing what to cite; it just couldn't write bindable prose when its menu was crammed with them.

That leaves depth as the whole game, and **Rank7 is the lever built for it and never run**. Let me confirm the exact env var names from the committed code, then launch it on the clean pool.

content.text:
No contradiction — the other bands are all inside the **Mechanism-section rule**, which is gated on `section title == "Mechanism"` (we have no such section), and the `6-8` is rhetorical ("do NOT stop at 6-8"). Both live copies of rule #8 moved to 28-36, including the back-reference. The lever is clean — verified, not assumed.

Now let me record the A/B findings and get Fable gating them while the depth run composes.

input.command:
cat >> /home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md <<'EOF'

- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **CLEAN-POOL A/B LANDED — AND IT KILLED THE PAYOFF EVERYONE (INCLUDING ME) PREDICTED.** Both arms same corpus/task/seed-space, judge ON vs OFF. CTRL(dirty): 10 sections, **3408 words**, 81 verified / 75 dropped, 63 bib entries. ARMED(clean, 559 rows deleted): 10 sections, **4489 words (+32%)**, **104 verified / 68 dropped**, 72 bib entries. Judge landed **507 deletable AGAIN — bit-identical to the prior independent run**.
- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **THE RELEVANCE PAYOFF IS ~ZERO — MY OWN 21:40 THESIS IS DEAD.** I argued (and Fable half-endorsed) that a ~51%-alien menu poisons shipped prose, so the clean pool's prize is RELEVANCE, i.e. a RACE win. **Measured it instead of assuming: the dirty report's shipped bibliography is 1.6% alien — ONE row (ev_852) out of 63.** The armed report: 0 of 72. **A ~51%-alien POOL yields a ~1.6%-alien BIBLIOGRAPHY** — the section writer was already discarding alien rows at citation time. So the relevance prize is worth *one citation*. Both the "poisoned prose" story and the RACE-relevance payoff are **refuted by the data**, and they were mine.
- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **FABLE'S PREDICTION IS *NOT* REFUTED — AND I ALMOST CLAIMED IT WAS.** Fable predicted |Δkept_fraction| <= 0.05 (strict_verify is topicality-blind). Observed 0.519 -> 0.605 = **+0.085**, and my comparator printed "PREDICTION BROKEN". **That claim does not survive arithmetic:** kept_fraction is a proportion over ~160 sentence trials; SE(diff) = sqrt(2 * 0.55*0.45/160) ≈ **0.055**, so +0.085 is **~1.5σ — indistinguishable from noise at n=1 per arm.** A single-run aggregate cannot overturn a source-verified structural fact (`judge(sentence, span)` never receives the question). **Recording this as UNRESOLVED, not as a win.** (Same discipline as Rank6: an aggregate is not a mechanism.)
- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **WHAT THE CLEAN POOL ACTUALLY BOUGHT: the WRITER, not the VERIFIER.** Repair-loop success **0.71 (dirty) -> 0.87 (clean)**; verified sentences 81 -> 104. Reconciles both facts without contradiction: strict_verify really is topicality-blind, *and* pool cleanliness still raises survival — **via the writer, not the judge.** Forced to build an on-topic section from an alien row, the writer writes a sentence that **overreaches its span** (an unsupported bridge) and entailment fails. Fable's inference "topicality therefore cannot affect kept_fraction" carried a hidden assumption — **that the sentences written are the same regardless of pool. They are not.** Cleaning the pool changes *what gets written*, not *how it is judged.
- 2026-07-12 22:15 | Rank6a LEAK MEASURED, NOT ASSUMED: armed cites **0 off-subject rows** — the back-door fold-in did not re-import any judged-alien row into the shipped bibliography this run. The hole is still structurally real (`dropped_rows=[]` hard-coded) and must be armed before any retrieval-side depth push, but it is **not currently leaking into the report**. Downgraded from "load-bearing now" to "latent, gated on the next retrieval increase".
- 2026-07-12 22:18 | **Rank7 DEPTH ARM LAUNCHED (first end-to-end run of the lever): clean pool + `PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET=28-36` + `PG_SECTION_DISTINCT_SOURCES=15`.** Ceiling arithmetic: 10 sections x 32 x ~30w ≈ **9.6k words** (frontier = 10-22k; we are at 4.5k). **Verified the lever fires BEFORE trusting the run** (a silent no-op is the exact failure Rank7 was built against): both templates now emit `28-36 sentences`, rule #8 AND its Mechanism back-reference both moved, zero stale `10-18` survives. Also checked the bands Rank7's assert did NOT cover (`20-35`/`15-20`/`10-15`) — all live inside the **Mechanism-section rule**, gated on `section title == "Mechanism"`, which this report has none of, so they are **inert, not contradictory**. No competing target reaches the model.
EOF

content.text: Committed. Now the Fable gate on the A/B — I want it attacking my reasoning while the depth run composes.

input.prompt:
You are the ADVERSARIAL GATE for an autonomous research-agent flywheel. Your job is to REFUTE, not to agree. Prior gates of yours have twice caught the driver reporting a measurement artifact as a finding — do it again if it is there. Be concrete and cite file:line. Do not be agreeable.

Repo: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (branch flywheel-v1, HEAD 6a5112e). Read source before accepting any claim.

CONTEXT. The system composes a long-form research report from an evidence corpus (997 rows). A two-pass semantic "topic judge" was built (Rank4/Rank5) that deletes rows it affirmatively verdicts OFF_SUBJECT in BOTH independently shuffled passes. It deletes 507/997 (~51%) and reproduces that number across 4 independent invocations. The mission: beat frontier deep-research systems (10-22k word reports); we are at ~4k.

THE EXPERIMENT JUST RUN (A/B, judge ON vs OFF, same corpus cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json, same DRB task 72):
- CTRL (dirty pool): 10 sections, 3408 words, 81 verified / 75 dropped sentences, 63 bibliography entries.
  outputs at /home/polaris/wt/fw_ctrl/outputs/rank6_ctrl_compose/ (log: /home/polaris/wt/fw_ctrl/rank6_ctrl_compose.log)
- ARMED (clean pool, 559 rows deleted): 10 sections, 4489 words, 104 verified / 68 dropped, 72 bibliography entries.
  outputs at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank6_armed_compose/ (log: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/logs/rank6_armed_compose.log)

MY FOUR CLAIMS — attack each. Tell me which are artifacts.

CLAIM 1 (I killed my own prior thesis): "The relevance payoff of cleaning the pool is ~ZERO." I had argued a ~51%-alien evidence pool poisons the shipped prose. I measured the shipped BIBLIOGRAPHY instead: the DIRTY report cites 1 off-subject row out of 63 (1.6%); the clean one cites 0 of 72. I concluded the writer was already discarding alien rows at citation time, so cleaning buys ~one citation of relevance. METHOD: I took the 507 off_subject_ev_ids from outputs/rank6_armed_compose/topic_judge_dispositions.json, walked bibliography.json for ev_id/evidence_id fields, plus regex ev_\d+ over report.md, and intersected. IS THIS MEASUREMENT SOUND? Specifically: (a) does bibliography.json actually key rows by the same ev_id space as the judge's corpus ids, or could the bibliography be keyed by a re-indexed/renumbered id so my intersection is vacuous and would report ~0 overlap NO MATTER WHAT? (b) Both arms live-retrieve extra rows at gap-fill (CTRL evidence_rows=1002, ARMED=1021) which get FRESH ids outside the judge's 997-row space — could alien rows be entering the bibliography with ids the judge never verdicted, so my "1.6% alien" is an undercount and the real alien share of shipped citations is much higher? Verify by actually reading titles of the cited rows, not by trusting my number. If my relevance conclusion is wrong the whole prioritisation flips, so this is the most important thing you check.

CLAIM 2 (I refused to claim a win): Fable (you, earlier) predicted |delta kept_fraction| <= 0.05 because strict_verify is topicality-blind (provenance_generator.py:3598 never takes the research question; entailment_judge.py:740 judge(sentence, span) is prompted only "Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE"). Observed 0.519 -> 0.605 = +0.085. I refused to call this a refutation: kept_fraction is a proportion over ~160 sentence trials, SE(diff) ~ sqrt(2*0.55*0.45/160) ~ 0.055, so +0.085 is ~1.5 sigma = noise at n=1 per arm. IS MY STATISTICS RIGHT? Is the binomial model even appropriate (sentences within a section are NOT independent draws — they share a menu, a prompt, and a repair loop; does clustering make my SE too SMALL, i.e. is the true uncertainty even bigger)? Am I right to log this UNRESOLVED rather than as a win?

CLAIM 3 (my reconciliation): "The clean pool helps the WRITER, not the VERIFIER." Evidence: repair-loop success 0.71 -> 0.87, verified sentences 81 -> 104. Mechanism I propose: strict_verify really is topicality-blind, but when the writer is forced to build an ON-TOPIC section from an ALIEN row, it writes a sentence that OVERREACHES its span (an unsupported bridge), and entailment then fails on faithfulness grounds — no topic-awareness required. So your earlier inference ("topicality cannot affect kept_fraction") carried a hidden assumption: that the sentences WRITTEN are the same regardless of pool. IS THIS MECHANISM REAL OR A JUST-SO STORY? Can you find direct evidence in the logs (dropped sentence texts, repair_loop lines, per-section kept_fraction) that discriminates it from the alternative explanation: that the +32% word gain is simply MENU SIZE — multi_section_generator.py:513-541 records that oversized writer menus (52-103 rows) caused a kept_fraction collapse to 0.35, and PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION (:541, the purpose-built menu cap) has NEVER been switched on. If it is menu size, then the cheap lever is the cap (free) and NOT the 42-minute judge, and my whole ranking is wrong.

CLAIM 4 (the ceiling lever, now running): Rank7 (commit 33108d8) made the section sentence target tunable: PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET / PG_SECTION_DISTINCT_SOURCES applied at the single _select_section_system_prompt seam (multi_section_generator.py:3903). I verified both templates emit "28-36 sentences", both copies of rule #8 moved, no stale "10-18" survives, and the other bands (20-35/15-20/10-15) are inert because they are inside the Mechanism-section rule gated on section title == "Mechanism" (no such section here). A run is live now with 28-36 / 15 distinct sources on the clean pool. WHAT WILL BREAK? Consider specifically: B11 same-span dedup (verified_compose.dedup_same_span_sentences) keeps only ONE sentence per distinct (ev_id,start,end) span — so if sections do not HAVE 15+ distinct usable sources, does raising the sentence target just produce sentences that get dedup'd or dropped, making the report SHORTER (the project already measured "more rows => thinner prose")? Is there a per-section kept_fraction FLOOR with retry (I saw "post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.31 below min 0.40 — retrying") that will now thrash or burn budget? Predict the failure mode CONCRETELY and give me the number to look at first when it lands.

Return: a verdict per claim (CONFIRMED / REFUTED / ARTIFACT / UNPROVEN), the file:line evidence, and — most important — the ONE measurement I should make next that would most change the ranking of what to do. Be brutal.

# ADVERSARIAL GATE VERDICT — Rank6 A/B + Rank7

## CLAIM 1 — "Relevance payoff of cleaning is ~zero": **CONFIRMED** (independently recomputed; your method had two latent holes that happened not to bite)

I did not trust your intersection — I rebuilt it padding-safe from raw files.

**(a) Id-space vacuity — NO.** The corpus (`cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json`, 997 rows) uses ids `ev_000`–`ev_1270` (992 ev_N + 5 slugs like `acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks`). All 507 `off_subject_ev_ids` in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank6_armed_compose/topic_judge_dispositions.json` resolve into that corpus (507/507 after normalization). Bibliography top-level `evidence_id` is in the SAME space (59/63 CTRL, 67/72 ARMED are ev_N in corpus). Not renumbered. My independent, zero-pad-normalized intersection reproduces your numbers exactly: **CTRL cites 1 off-subject row (entry [46], ev_852, "How Interest Groups Influence Public Policy — UMSV Online", cited once in prose), ARMED cites 0.** Two method holes you got away with: (i) your `ev_\d+` regex over report.md was structurally vacuous — prose uses `[n]` markers, and both summaries record `leaked_cite_ev_tokens: 0`, so that leg of your method could never find anything; (ii) the corpus zero-pads ids under 1000 (`ev_050`) — a raw string intersection would silently miss any unpadded variant. Neither changed the answer this time. Say so in the log.

**(b) Fresh-row undercount — REAL but SMALL, and it cuts AGAINST the clean arm.** Corpus max is ev_1270. CTRL cites 1 unjudged fresh row (ev_1271, on-topic title). ARMED cites **3** (ev_1283, ev_1290, ev_1291). So the "much higher hidden alien share" fear is refuted — ceiling is 3/72 = 4%. BUT: read ARMED bibliography entries #1 and #2. ev_1290's verified "claim_text" is a chopped reference-list scrap (`"on and sustainable growth. _Technol. Forecast. Soc. Change_ 162, 120398..."`) marked ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED/SUPPORTS; ev_1291 is `basket_verdict: "unverified"` (weight 0.45) and is still bibliography entry #2, cited in prose. **The clean report's two lead citations are garbage-quality gap-fill rows the judge never saw.** Your relevance metric is blind to this; it is a quality problem, not a topicality problem, and it shipped in the ARMED arm.

Title-level audit of all 63 CTRL entries: only ev_852 is alien. Your 1.6% stands under direct inspection. The prioritisation flip you feared is not there — the payoff conclusion holds.

## CLAIM 2 — "+0.085 is unresolved noise": **CONFIRMED, and your SE is too SMALL — plus a bias you missed**

Premise verified in source: `strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...)` takes no research question (`src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:3598`); `judge(self, sentence: str, span: str)` at `src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:741` sees only sentence+span. Counts verified: CTRL 81/156 = 0.519, ARMED 104/172 = 0.605.

Your binomial SE (~0.055) is a **lower bound**. Sentences cluster within 10 sections sharing a menu, a draft, and a repair loop — with any positive ICC the design effect inflates the true SE, so +0.085 is even less than your 1.5σ.

The bias you missed: the retry floor **selects the better of two drafts** (`multi_section_generator.py:6593`: `if len(report2_kept_after_m41c) > post_filter_kept`). Per the logs, ARMED fired this best-of-two retry in **3 sections** (first-pass kept_fractions 0.38, 0.31, 0.29 — `logs/rank6_armed_compose.log:2076,2087,2121`); CTRL fired it **zero** times. Part of the +0.085 is mechanical retry selection applied asymmetrically. UNRESOLVED is the right log entry; if anything the delta is inflated.

Note what those 3 ARMED retries also tell you: **the clean pool still collapsed below the 0.40 floor in 3 of 10 sections.** Cleanliness did not fix the kept_fraction failure mode.

## CLAIM 3 — "Clean pool helps the WRITER": **ARTIFACT-RISK / UNPROVEN — the menu-size confound is live and unmeasured, and it is large**

The decisive numbers, from the two `multi_section_outline.json` files — per-section writer menus:

| | CTRL | ARMED |
|---|---|---|
| menus | 99, 195, 82, 45, 45, 34, 33, 12, 52, **358** | 45, 61, 39, 70, 37, 24, 8, 11, 14, **156** |

The judge halved the menus. The project's own prior measurement (`multi_section_generator.py:521-525`) says menus of 52-103 rows alone — no alien pool involved — collapse kept_fraction to 0.35 and thin prose. CTRL ran 4 sections above that band, one at 358 rows. And the 120K char-budget did NOT save it: zero `char_budget_exceeded` / "char-budget bound" lines in either log (the budget is only applied `if _redesign`, line 2276/2653 — it was 0 here), so the writer genuinely saw 195- and 358-row menus. **Your A/B changed pool cleanliness and menu size simultaneously. It cannot discriminate your overreach mechanism from menu overload.** I found no sentence-level evidence for the overreach story either — drop-reason lines in the logs are too sparse (8 verify_fail + 4 no_provenance_token visible in CTRL) to support it. It is currently a just-so story sitting on top of a confound, and the confounded variable has the stronger prior — measured, in this codebase, at this effect size. `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (`multi_section_generator.py:541`) remains OFF and free.

## CLAIM 4 — Rank7 prediction: **code CONFIRMED, outcome will be dedup/floor-limited in tail sections — here is the concrete failure**

Code verified: `_apply_depth_targets` (`multi_section_generator.py:~3905`) rewrites both anchors with `re.subn` count-enforced fail-loud, including the Mechanism back-reference. The seam is sound.

The failure mode: ARMED-shaped tail sections have menus of **8, 11, 14 rows** (Policy, Cross-Study, Conclusions). Rule #9 will demand 15 distinct sources from an 8-row menu — unsatisfiable by construction. The writer will pad 28-36 sentences by re-citing the same spans; B11 (`verified_compose.py:155`, keep-one-per-(ev_id,start,end)-footprint, number carve-out only) eats the rewords. At the 10-18 target B11 collapsed only 1 sentence per run (logs, both arms); at 28-36 it becomes the binding constraint in small-menu sections. Then post-M-41c kept_fraction lands below 0.40 and the floor fires — but it is a **single** retry with best-of-two, not a thrash loop (6538-6593; `regen_attempted` once), so budget burn is bounded at ~2x generation per section, not runaway.

**Concrete prediction:** rich sections (37-70 rows) gain real length; the 3 tail sections plateau at roughly menu-size-to-2x-menu-size kept sentences regardless of target. Expect total verified ~140-170, dropped sentences roughly doubling, and 4-6 "below min 0.40 — retrying" lines. **The number to read first when it lands:** the count of `B11 same-span dedup: collapsed N` per section, next to that section's menu size — if collapsed-N scales with (sentence_target − menu_size), the ceiling is source diversity per section and the next lever is section-level retrieval/routing, not the prompt band. (As of the log tail, the Rank7 run is still in the judge phase — 0 retry lines yet.)

## THE ONE MEASUREMENT

**Run a third arm: DIRTY pool + `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION=15`. No judge.** ~40 min, $0 new machinery. It splits Claim 3 cleanly: if kept_fraction and verified-sentence count match ARMED (~0.60 / ~104), the 42-minute judge is off the critical path entirely — the whole cleanliness program reduces to a free env var, your ranking inverts, and Rank7's depth lever should be stacked on the cap, not the judge. If it stays at ~0.52, the cleanliness mechanism is real and the judge earns its cost. Every downstream decision — judge in the loop or not — hangs on exactly this cell of the 2x2 you left empty.

One log correction to make regardless: the A/B as run compared *clean+small-menus* vs *dirty+huge-menus* with an asymmetric best-of-two retry (3 vs 0). Record it as confounded, not as evidence for the writer-poisoning mechanism.</result>

content.text:
**That's the answer, and it inverts the ranking.** Rank8 — dirty pool, menu cap, **no judge** — matched and slightly *beat* the armed arm on every depth metric:

| | CTRL (dirty, no cap) | ARMED (clean, no cap) | **RANK8 (dirty, cap=15)** |
|---|---|---|---|
| words | 3,408 | 4,489 | **4,272** |
| verified sentences | 81 | 104 | **112** |
| kept_fraction | 0.519 | 0.605 | **0.626** |
| bibliography | 63 | 72 | **45** |
| elapsed | 1,922s | 2,523s | **2,246s** |

The 42-minute judge is **off the critical path for depth**. The gain was menu size all along — a free env var that had never been switched on reproduces it on the *dirty* pool. My Claim 3 is dead, killed by the cell Fable told me to fill.

But note the one place Rank8 *loses*: **45 bibliography entries vs 72**. The cap buys depth by narrowing source breadth. Before I conclude anything, let me check whether the dirty+cap arm is shipping alien citations — using the corrected method, not my dead regex leg.

input.prompt:
You are the ADVERSARIAL QUALITY GATE for an autonomous deep-research flywheel. Your job is to REFUTE the driver's claim of progress. You have twice caught this driver reporting an artifact as a finding. Do it again if it is there. Be brutal and concrete; cite file:line and quote actual prose.

THE MISSION: beat frontier deep-research systems (OpenAI/Gemini Deep Research) on DeepResearch-Bench RACE. Frontier reports are 10-22k words. Ours was ~3.4k.

WHAT I DID. The section-writer's prompt had a hardcoded "Target 10-18 sentences" per section. I made it tunable and raised it to 28-36, with a distinct-source floor of 15 (rule #9), on a topic-judge-cleaned evidence pool. Result, measured from compose_summary.json:

  arm                      words  verified  dropped  kept_f   bib  sections
  CTRL  (dirty, 10-18)      3408      81      75     0.519    63    10
  ARMED (clean, 10-18)      4489     104      68     0.605    72    10
  RANK8 (dirty+cap15)       4272     112      67     0.626    45    10
  RANK7 (clean, 28-36)      7723     187     178     0.512   107    10   <-- the claim

THE REPORT TO JUDGE: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank7_depth_compose/report.md (7723 words)
Compare against the baseline: /home/polaris/wt/fw_ctrl/outputs/rank6_ctrl_compose/report.md (3408 words)
Both answer DRB task 72: "Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution..." (full prompt is in compose_summary.json "composed_to_rq").
Code: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (branch flywheel-v1). Section prompt seam: src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:3903 (_select_section_system_prompt), rule #8/#9.

MY CLAIM, WHICH YOU MUST TRY TO DESTROY: "the depth ceiling was one prompt line; raising it produced a 2.27x longer report that is genuinely deeper — more verified sentences (187 vs 81) and MORE distinct sources cited (107 vs 63), not padding."

ATTACK IT ON THESE AXES. Read the actual prose, do not reason from the summary numbers.

1. IS IT PADDING? Read RANK7's sections against CTRL's. Is the extra length (a) new INFORMATION — distinct findings, distinct studies, quantitative results, mechanisms, contradictions — or (b) restatement, hedging, throat-clearing, transition sentences, and the same fact re-attributed to more sources? Quote the worst offending passages verbatim. A 2.27x length gain that is 50% filler is a RACE LOSS, not a win: RACE penalizes redundancy and rewards insight density. Give me a rough estimate of what fraction of RANK7's added words carry new information.

2. REDUNDANCY / REPETITION. The generator has a same-span dedup (B11) that removes sentences re-citing an identical (ev_id,start,end) span, and a fact_dedup pass. Those only catch EXACT span reuse. At 28-36 sentences/section the model may restate the same FINDING in different words citing DIFFERENT sources — invisible to both dedups. Search RANK7 for near-duplicate claims (e.g. the same displacement/augmentation statistic or the same "AI complements high-skill workers" claim restated across sections). Count them. Is the report saying the same 20 things 187 times?

3. COHERENCE AND STRUCTURE. Does the longer report still read as ONE review with an argument, or as 10 disconnected walls of citation? Is there a real cross-study synthesis (agreements AND contradictions), or does the synthesis section just list studies? Frontier reports are graded on comprehensiveness, insight, instruction-following, and readability — score RANK7 on each 1-5 vs CTRL and justify.

4. THE 178 DROPPED SENTENCES (kept_fraction fell 0.605 -> 0.512). The writer wrote 365 sentences and strict_verify killed 178. Is that a warning sign I am ignoring — i.e. at 28-36 the model is now HALLUCINATING/overreaching and only the verifier is saving us? Or is it healthy over-generation? What in the logs (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/logs/rank7_depth_compose.log) tells them apart? Note the per-section kept_fraction floor (min 0.40) fired only ONCE.

5. WHAT IS THE NEXT REAL CEILING? We are at 7.7k; frontier floor is 10k. Sections are fixed at 10 while the facet outline allows up to 40. Is "more sections" the right next lever, or will it fragment the review? Is raising 28-36 higher the right lever, or is source diversity per section already the binding constraint (bib=107 over 10 sections = ~10.7 distinct sources/section against a rule-#9 floor of 15 — is rule #9 already UNSATISFIABLE, and what happens when a rule is unsatisfiable)? Give me the ONE next lever, and the ONE number that would falsify it.

Return: verdict on my claim (CONFIRMED / PARTLY / REFUTED / ARTIFACT), a padding-fraction estimate with quoted evidence, RACE-dimension scores for both reports, and the single highest-value next action. Do not be agreeable — if this report is a padded regression dressed up as a 2.27x win, say so plainly and prove it.

<task-notification>
<task-id>a2890f36a39c7b28a</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01MasbE8dfwtnB85KDKW8pEP</tool-use-id>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Fable gate: is Rank7 padded?" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>VERDICT: **PARTLY** — the direction is real, but two of your three headline numbers are inflated, your claimed mechanism (rule #9) demonstrably did not operate, and roughly a third of the added words are not new information. This is not an artifact and not a padded regression; it is a genuine depth gain wearing exaggerated numbers.

## 1. The headline numbers, corrected

**"107 vs 63 sources" is bibliography-entry inflation, not source diversity.** The 107 bib entries collapse to 92 unique URLs, 82 markers actually cited in prose, and ~73 distinct works by title-matching — itself still inflated by mirror-splitting. Brynjolfsson/Li/Raymond alone occupies **six** bib slots in RANK7 ([59],[60],[61],[97],[98],[99] — report.md:109-111,147-149); "GPTs are GPTs" occupies five ([26]-[29],[102]); the Hui et al. short-term-effects paper four ([42]-[45]). CTRL: 63 entries → 48 works, 40 cited. Real ratio is ~73 vs ~40 distinct works — the direction holds, the number doesn't.

**The killer counter-metric: distinct-works-per-1000-words FELL, ~11.8 (CTRL) → ~9.5 (RANK7).** Insight density per word declined. RACE grades density, not totals.

**"Rule #9 drove diversity" is refuted by the report itself.** Distinct refs per section in RANK7's final text: 7, 8, 17, 5, 13, 8, 8, 7, 8, 8. The 15-distinct-source floor is met in **1 of 10 sections**. "Empirical Evidence on Employment" has 17 verified sentences on **5** distinct refs. "Theoretical Foundations" has 28 sentences on 8 refs, **10 of them citing [1]** — one Acemoglu-Restrepo paper restated ten ways ("displacement effect… productivity effect… reinstatement effect… present such a framework… builds on prior work… The net impact of automation on labor demand thus depends on how the displacement and productivity effects weigh against each other.[1]" — report.md:11). Rule #9 at 15 is unsatisfiable against current per-section evidence slices, and the observed behavior when a rule is unsatisfiable is exactly what you feared: the model silently ignores it and pads from the sources it has, in span-distinct rewordings B11 cannot see.

## 2. Padding fraction: ~30-35% of the ADDED words; ~20-25% of the whole report

Of 187 verified sentences, I count ~40-45 that carry no new information: cross-section duplicates (~20), metadata-as-content (~8), off-topic strays (~7), broken fragments (2), plus content-free wrap-ups. Worst offenders, verbatim:

**Cross-section near-duplicates (the B11-invisible mode, confirmed present):**
- [90] stated twice, verbatim-grade: "A systematic literature review synthesizes the multifaceted nature of AI's employment impact, noting new opportunities from high-skilled job creation… alongside challenges from low-skilled job replacement and employment structure polarization.[90]" (report.md:35) vs "A systematic literature review concluded that the impact of AI on employment is multifaceted, with new opportunities arising from the emergence of high-skilled jobs… alongside challenges from the replacement of low-skilled jobs and polarization of the employment structure.[90]" (report.md:39).
- Hamilton [86] "inconclusive… claims about harmful impacts on particular groups of workers are premature" — report.md:35 AND report.md:39, near-verbatim, plus a third paraphrase in the same Conclusions paragraph.
- CBO "direction, size, and timing… remain uncertain, with some budgetary effects occurring relatively quickly and others taking longer"[89] — report.md:35 AND :39.
- MIT Sloan 14% task-level claim — report.md:7, again :27, thesis restated a third time :39.

  - **MY DEDUP DEFENCE WAS FALSE COMFORT — AND MY OWN LOG DISPROVES IT** (rank7_depth_compose.log:4310): `fact_dedup: groups=16 redundants=20 rewrites_proposed=20 rewrites_kept=1 rewrites_dropped_by_strict_verify=19`. The pipeline **detected 16 duplicate-fact groups**, tried 20 consolidations, and **strict_verify killed 19 of 20 — the consolidation pass no-ops (1/20)**. B11 fired **once** all run. Every duplicate below sailed through both.
  - **PADDING ~30-35% of the ADDED words** (~20-25% of the whole). Cross-section verbatim-grade dupes ([90] and Hamilton [86] and CBO [89] each stated twice, in Synthesis AND Conclusions); **different-source dupes invisible to every dedup we have** (Eloundou 46% via [26] and again via [102]; ILO 9.6%/3.5% via [33] and via [95]); **verified-but-VACUOUS metadata-as-content** — *"This occupational, industry, and geographic exposure dataset was published in the Strategic Management Journal, volume 42, issue 12, in December 2021.[22]"*, *"The study received 239 Scopus citations.[22]"*, Korinek cited to a **Google Scholar profile page**. **strict_verify checks grounding, NOT informativeness** — that is the hole vacuous sentences walk through.
  - **READABILITY REGRESSED (2.5 -> 2) on shipped ARTIFACTS**: **27 `(also mirrored)` markers leaked into prose** (CTRL: 3); **two sections open MID-WORD** — *"ings in the last two years.[85]"*, *"stionnaire developed by the researchers.[100][101]"*; and **fact_dedup leaked its own cross-reference into the report body**: *"The Brynjolfsson, Li, and Raymond customer-support agent study is detailed under Productivity Gains from Generative AI at Work.[97][98]"*
  - **INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING MISS THAT SHIPPED:** DRB task 72 says *"AI, as a key driver of the **Fourth Industrial Revolution**"*. That phrase appears **ZERO times** in either report body. **Our own outline checklist flagged it as a gap (log:1342) and it shipped unaddressed.** Free RACE points, lost.
  - RACE (Fable, 1-5): comprehensiveness **2.5 -> 4**, insight **2.5 -> 3**, instruction-following **3 -> 3**, readability **2.5 -> 2**.
  - **HONEST RESTATEMENT OF THE WIN: ~1.9x INFORMATION gain, not 2.27x words. ~73 distinct works, not 107. Density DOWN.**
- 2026-07-12 23:35 | **NEXT LEVER (Fable, and I agree): the binding constraint is DISTINCT USABLE EVIDENCE PER SECTION, not sentence count.** Raising 28-36 higher is exhausted — tail kept_fraction is already 0.36 (conclusions/additional), fact_dedup is already detecting duplicate-fact groups, rule #9 is already ignored 9/10 times. The lever is **MORE SECTIONS (13-14) WITH DISJOINT PER-SECTION EVIDENCE ALLOCATION** — give each section its own basket slice so 28-36 sentences draw on ~15 genuinely distinct works instead of milking 8. 13 x ~750w clears 10k. **THE PRE-REGISTERED FALSIFIER: distinct-works-cited per 1000 words. RANK7 = 9.5, CTRL = 11.8. If the next run lands ABOVE ~11 at 10k+ words the lever worked; if it stays <=9.5 I bought words with restatement again and the lever is WRONG.**
EOF

content.content:
1	"""Cross-section repetition guard (I-deepfix-001 FIX 5, GH #1344).
2	
3	THE DEFECT (composition-collapse audit, drb_72): the composer recycled ~8 findings across 6
4	sections (e.g. Goldman's 2.5% GDP estimate appeared in 4 sections, the robot-count estimate in
5	4, the "15% of tasks" figure in 3, the Frey-Osborne method in 4). ``fact_dedup`` consolidates
6	near-restatements WITHIN a single section only, so a finding restated VERBATIM in a DIFFERENT
7	section survives — burning section space that DISTINCT findings should occupy.
8	
9	THE GUARD (this module): after per-section compose, detect a finding (a cited sentence) that
10	recurs VERBATIM across DIFFERENT sections and CONSOLIDATE it to its richest instance plus a short
11	citation-preserving back-reference, freeing section space for distinct findings.
12	
13	§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL (never a drop / cap / thin): every recycled instance KEEPS its own
14	numeric citation marker(s) inline on the back-reference — no citation is ever dropped, and NO
15	citation is moved between sections (so the per-section-local citation numbering the downstream
16	``_remap_section_markers_to_global`` relies on stays valid). Repetition is consolidated, not
17	deleted; the finding's full prose remains in its richest section.
18	
19	EXACT-RECYCLE ONLY — NOT a paraphrase-collapse (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #2): equivalence is an
20	EXACT normalized-text match (NUMERIC-citation-stripped, whitespace-collapsed, lowercased,
21	trailing-punctuation-stripped). Two sentences that differ in ANY content — a year, a figure, an
22	entity, directionality, an extra clause — have DIFFERENT normalized text and are NEVER clustered.
23	Crucially, ONLY renderer-guaranteed numeric citation markers (``[12]``) are stripped from the
24	signature; NON-citation bracketed content — a bracketed entity/label such as ``[Alpha]`` — is
25	PRESERVED in the signature (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1). So ``The model [Alpha] cut error 10% [1]``
26	and ``The model [Beta] cut error 10% [2]`` have DIFFERENT signatures and are never clustered:
27	distinct verified content can NEVER be replaced by a back-reference. A loose token-overlap
28	threshold is deliberately NOT used — it would let near-identical-but-distinct sentences cluster,
29	turning a render-only consolidation into a silent DROP (a §-1.3 violation).
30	
31	MARKER-REQUIRED — never rewrites an honest gap disclosure (Fable diff-gate iter-1 P1): a unit is an
32	eligible finding ONLY if it carries at least one NUMERIC ``[N]`` citation marker. Every
33	strict_verify-kept claim sentence carries markers (§9.1 invariant 2); the production gap / degraded
34	disclosures (``_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE`` / ``_NO_EVIDENCE_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE`` /
35	``_SECTION_FAILED_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE``) and back-references are marker-less BY DESIGN. So two starved
36	sections carrying the same gap stub can NEVER cluster, and the honest per-section gap disclosure is
37	never deleted or replaced by a false "See ... for this finding" pointer. Belt-and-braces: a section
38	flagged ``is_gap_stub=True`` is skipped entirely.
39	
40	LAYOUT-PRESERVING (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #3): a recycled instance is replaced by an in-place
41	single-occurrence substring swap on the ORIGINAL ``verified_text`` — every other byte (markdown
42	``### slot`` sub-headings, blank lines, adjacent sentences) is preserved unchanged. The guard NEVER
43	re-splits + ``" ".join``-rejoins a body (which would flatten ``### Heading`` layout into inline
44	prose and corrupt contract-section slot headings). A unit whose text contains a markdown heading
45	line (``#``-prefixed) is not eligible, and a unit that does not occur EXACTLY once in its body is
46	left untouched — precision-first: never risk corrupting real prose on a splitter/matching miss.
47	
48	FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL (constraint 1, never a relax): RENDER-ONLY. It edits ONLY the rendered
49	``verified_text`` string, and ONLY AFTER the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI
50	entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) has already run per section. It does not touch
51	``kept_sentences_pre_resolve`` (the SentenceVerification objects the 4-role D8 gate judges), the
52	per-section verified/dropped counts, or any evidence row.
53	
54	KILL-SWITCH (LAW VI / constraint 3): ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD`` — DEFAULT OFF. When OFF
55	(or unset) the guard is a no-op and the assembled report is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the legacy path.
56	Tunable: ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_MIN_WORDS`` (min distinct content words per eligible finding,
57	default 6) — read from the environment (no magic numbers).
58	"""
59	from __future__ import annotations
60	
61	import logging
62	import os
63	import re
64	from typing import Any
65	
66	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.cross_section_repetition_guard")
67	
68	# ── Environment knobs (LAW VI — every threshold/flag is env-tunable, no magic numbers) ──────────
69	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD"
70	_ENV_MIN_WORDS = "PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_MIN_WORDS"
71	
72	_MIN_WORDS_DEFAULT = 6
73	
74	_OFF_TOKENS = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
75	
76	# NUMERIC citation marker ONLY (``[1]``, ``[12]``). This is the renderer-guaranteed citation syntax
77	# at this pre-global-remap stage (§9.1 invariant 2). It is the ONLY thing stripped from the
78	# equivalence signature and is what a unit's eligibility ("is this a strict_verify-kept CLAIM
79	# sentence?") is keyed on. NON-citation bracketed content (a bracketed entity/label such as
80	# ``[Alpha]``) is deliberately NOT matched here, so it stays IN the signature and two units that
81	# differ only by such an entity are never clustered (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1 — no faithfulness leak).
82	_CITATION_NUM_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
83	
84	# Content-word tokenizer for the min-content-words eligibility gate ONLY (never the equivalence
85	# signature). Unicode-aware (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P2): ``[^\W_]+`` matches runs of Unicode letters
86	# and digits (excluding underscore), so a repeated cited finding written in a non-Latin script is
87	# still measured by real content-word count instead of silently underfiring an ASCII-only ``[a-z0-9]``.
88	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_]+", re.UNICODE)
89	
90	# Function words removed ONLY for the min-content-words eligibility count (never for the equivalence
91	# signature, which is an EXACT match). Intentionally small + generic.
92	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
93	    "a", "an", "and", "are", "as", "at", "be", "been", "but", "by", "for", "from", "had", "has",
94	    "have", "in", "into", "is", "it", "its", "of", "on", "or", "that", "the", "their", "them",
95	    "these", "they", "this", "to", "was", "were", "which", "will", "with", "would",
96	})
97	
98	# The back-reference that replaces a recycled instance. The recycled instance's own numeric
99	# citation marker(s) are appended so no citation is dropped (§-1.3 keep-all).
100	_BACKREF_TEMPLATE = 'See "{title}" for this finding.'
101	_BACKREF_FALLBACK_TITLE = "the section above"
102	
103	
104	def guard_enabled() -> bool:
105	    """Kill-switch ``PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD`` — DEFAULT OFF.
